executionArgs.additionalInstruction:
Project: Respect App — Flutter web only, served at http://localhost:5173 from build/web (HTTP 200, dartvm.exe). 

CRITICAL ROUTING RULES:
1. Start only from http://localhost:5173/ — DO NOT navigate directly to /login, /home, or any other route. Always begin with `page.goto("http://localhost:5173/")` and let the app's AuthGate + onGenerateRoute redirect you. The app's initialRoute is '/', and the AuthGate will navigate to either LoginScreen, HomeScreen, or BlockedDeviceScreen after the splash finishes.
2. The app shows a SplashScreen with a logo and "Loading your world..." text first. Wait for that splash to complete and for the redirect to happen before interacting. Use generous waits (>= 20s) for the Flutter CanvasKit SPA to mount flt-glass-pane.
3. If a session token already exists in storage, the app lands on HomeScreen; otherwise on LoginScreen. Adapt the flow accordingly.

FEATURES TO TEST (in this exact order, all starting from /):
- LOGIN: enter credentials on the login form, submit, verify the home screen appears.
- REGISTER: switch to "حساب جديد" mode, fill the registration form (username, profile name, email, birth date, password, confirm password), accept terms, submit, verify the profile completion / feed flow.
- FEED: after login, verify posts are loaded in the feed and that the feed scrolls.
- CREATE POST: open the create-post composer, type content, submit, verify the new post appears in the feed.
- LIKE POST: tap the like button on a post, verify the like state changes and the count updates.
- SAVE POST: tap the save/bookmark button on a post, verify the saved state and that it appears in the saved-posts screen.
- COMMENTS AND REPLIES: open a post's comments, add a comment, reply to an existing comment, verify the thread updates.
- PROFILE: open the current user's profile from the drawer, verify avatar/name/cover/bio/posts are displayed.
- SEARCH: open the search screen, type a query, verify matching users and posts are returned.
- COMMUNITIES: open the communities/painters/streamers sections from the drawer, verify the listing renders.
- REPORTS: from a post, trigger the report flow, submit a report, verify the success state.
- NAVIGATION BUGS: traverse every drawer entry, every tab, every deep link; look for any broken navigation, white screens, or thrown exceptions.
- UI CRASHES: any red error overlay, flt-error, uncaught exception, or widget that fails to render should be reported as a FAIL.

IGNORE / SKIP:
- Android-only features: flutter_webrtc camera/mic capture, Firebase Cloud Messaging push, flutter_local_notifications system tray, CallActionHandler ringing/incoming-call UI. These are not supported on web; if a test for one of these is generated, mark it as BLOCKED with reason "android-only".
- Google Sign-In OAuth popup (inherently unreliable in headless browser; treat as BLOCKED).
- The 15-test dev-mode cap is fine; we do NOT need to push beyond it. Just run the selected 15.

ASSERTION STYLE:
- For UI states, use `await page.locator('flt-glass-pane').wait_for(state='visible', timeout=30000)` before any interaction.
- For navigation, check the URL or the visible Arabic content text (the UI is fully Arabic / RTL).
- Do NOT rely on hardcoded English strings.

TECHNICAL NOTES (already verified):
- The Flutter web build is in `build/web`, served by `dartvm.exe` on port 5173 (HTTP 200, body length 1245 bytes for /).
- The app uses Provider + Supabase + Firebase. There is no .env injected; assume the Supabase project is reachable.
- If a page ever shows a Reload button alone (the previous run's symptom), mark the test as BLOCKED and note "Flutter web SPA did not mount — flt-glass-pane never appeared after 30s".
